narrative:
DOMINANT DRIVERS

1. USD strength accelerating because hot US CPI at 3.8% is reinforcing H4 CSM score of 100 with DXY pushing higher intraday
2. CHF fading sharply because H4 CSM collapsed to 0 from D1 score of 100, the most extreme divergence across all currencies
3. NZD resilience persisting because NZD maintains top-tier scores on both timeframes at 89 and 68, confirmed by high correlation cluster with AUD and GBPUSD

PRICE CONFIRMS

USDCHF is the cleanest confirmation pair, with CHF fading on H4 while USD accelerates, and the -0.88 correlation to EURUSD means EUR weakness should follow but EURUSD shows a D1 buy signal creating a lag. NZDUSD and AUDUSD are moving together as expected given their +0.91 correlation, but the USD acceleration on H4 is compressing upside in both, meaning NZD strength is expressing more cleanly in crosses like NZDJPY. CADJPY leading USDJPY at +0.91 correlation confirms JPY weakness is broad and not pair-specific, consistent with BoJ intervention risk keeping the trade alive but not yet executing.

REGIME

Mixed, conflicted. VIX rising 1.58% to 18.67 signals creeping risk aversion, Gold is flat refusing to confirm safe-haven demand, and S&P 500 is up only marginally at 0.19%, making this an environment where USD strength is the dominant expression rather than a clean risk-off or risk-on regime.

CONTRADICTIONS

1. EURUSD signal vs CSM: D1 shows a buy signal on EURUSD but EUR H4 CSM at 66 with USD at 100 makes a sustained EUR bid structurally difficult on the shorter timeframe
2. Risk regime framing: news cites risk-off sentiment yet copper is up 1.29%, oil is up 2.69%, and equities are positive, contradicting a clean risk-off read

TRADE IMPLICATIONS

Best: USDCHF up - USD at H4 CSM peak, CHF collapsing from 100 to 0 on H4, cleanest divergence in the matrix - Conviction 8/10
Secondary: NZDJPY up - NZD holding strong on both timeframes, JPY under intervention threat with yen crosses confirming broad JPY weakness - Conviction 6/10
Avoid: EURUSD - D1 buy signal conflicts with H4 USD dominance creating directional paralysis
Watch: DXY breaking and holding above 98.50 confirms USD acceleration and validates the USDCHF long as the primary trade

themes.theme: US inflation hotter than expected at 3.8% vs 3.7% forecast
Japanese Yen intervention risk and BoJ rate hike expectations
British Pound weakness from UK political uncertainty
Euro strength versus GBP and CAD
Canadian Dollar weakness amid risk-off sentiment
Indian Rupee depreciation pressure from oil prices and FX defence concerns
Risk-off market sentiment with safe-haven demand
Oil prices elevated near 98 USD on Hormuz closure fears
Swiss Franc weakness despite SNB resistance to currency strength
US economic data mixed with weak NFIB sentiment offset by strong earnings

key_observation: US CPI surprise to 3.8% is the dominant driver supporting USD strength. Risk-off sentiment benefits safe havens JPY and CHF. GBP weakness from UK political turmoil and INR depreciation to all-time lows are notable underperformers. Oil elevated near 98 USD adds USD commodity currency pressure.
watch: BoJ intervention signals and rate hike timing. UK political developments and PM stability. Federal Reserve rate expectations revision post hot CPI. Iran tensions and Hormuz strait closure sustainability. Indian RBI FX intervention measures.
